description: Standard natural bids: one-level opening bids and natural notrump invite/game raises. This is an umbrella baseline вАФ responder actions to 1-of-a-suit openings and opener rebid continuations are intentionally out of scope for this module and are handled by dedicated response/opening modules.
purpose: Learn the baseline one-level opening bids and natural notrump raises that apply when no specialized convention changes the meaning of a call
scopeNote: Core SAYC natural opening / response / rebid framework used as the always-loaded base module: 1C / 1D / 1H / 1S openings (5-card majors, opener's rebid ranges 13-15 / 16-18 / 19-21), 1NT opening (15-17) with non-conventional escapes, and the `bridge.*` opening-selector facts that let convention modules override. `module.natural.notStrongTwoCHand` is clauses to every 1-level opening so Strong 2C outranks naturals when the hand qualifies. Direct 1NT jump continuations (invite-minor `3C` / `3D`, invite-major `3H` / `3S`, slam-invite `4NT`), the full `opened-suit` state entry for suit-opening continuations, Jacoby 2NT, and other convention-specific extensions are deferred to dedicated modules. Weak-2 and Strong-2C openings live in their own modules.

authority.label: ACBL вАФ SAYC System Booklet (SP3, rev. Jan 2006)

snapshot.text:
ACBL SAYC SYSTEM BOOKLET
                               Revised January 2006


                                    OVERVIEW
The ACBL Standard American Yellow Card (SAYC) was created to be the required
system to be used in a Standard Yellow Card event. The object was to provide a
simple, modern method that will lead to a good, solid understanding in a partnership
when both players have read this booklet.
Because this system was to be used by each pair, the game was free of a wide
variety of possibly complex systems. There was no necessity to Alert or ask
questions since everyone was playing the same method.
When play began on the Internet, players needing a quick way to establish the
method to be used in a relatively casual partnership adopted the SAYC. It is also
used by players who meet at the partnership desk a few minutes before the game
starts.
Players may still exercise bridge judgment, such as opening a four-card major in
third seat. SAYC вАЬnormally five-card majorsвАЭ approach can withstand an occasional
deviation.
                                     CHOICES
While there are no choices in the present day SAYC, except for an SAYC event in
which everyone must play the method as written, a pair could use the SAYC as a
base and make one or two modifications. If you do make a modification or two in
an open game, you should announce that fact to the opponents вАУ if they think you
are playing straight SAYC and they know SAYC, they would not know about your
modifications.
                              GENERAL APPROACH
Normally open five-card majors in all seats.
Open the higher of long suits of equal length: 5вАУ5 or 6вАУ6.
Normally open 1оБђ with 4вАУ4 in the minors.
Normally open 1оАІ with 3вАУ3 in the minors.
Notrump openings show a balanced hand and may be made with a five-card major
suit or a five-card minor suit.
1NT = 15вАУ17
2NT = 20вАУ21
3NT = 25вАУ27
Strong conventional 2оАІ opening.
Weak two-bids in diamonds, hearts and spades.

      RESPONSES AND LATER BIDDING AFTER A 15вАУ17 1NT OPENING
2оАІ is вАЬnon-forcingвАЭ Stayman, meaning that the bidding may stop in two of a suit.
Opener rebids 2оБЂ with 4вАУ4 in the majors. If responder rebids three of either minor,
he shows slam interest and at least a five-card suit.
The Jacoby transfer shows a five-card suit and is used for the majors: 2оБђ is a
transfer to hearts, 2оБЂ is a transfer to spades. Opener accepts the transfer, though
he can jump to the three level with 17 points and four-card support for responderвАЩs
major. For example:
    1NT вАФ 2оБђ
    2оБЂ = normal acceptance of the transfer.
     3оБЂ = 17 points and four-card heart support.
If, after the transfer is accepted, responder bids a new suit, that is natural and
game forcing. Possible calls after the accepted transfer are:
     1NT вАФ 2оБЂ
      2оАї вАФ Pass = content to play 2оАї.
		 вАФ 2NT, 3оАї = invitational. Over 2NT opener may pass or bid 3оАї
    			 with a minimum hand; bid 3NT or 4оАї with a maximum.
		 вАФ 3оАІ, 3оБђ, 3оБЂ = natural and game forcing.
		 вАФ 3NT = giving opener a choice between 3NT and 4оАї.
		 вАФ 4оАї = placing the contract, with a six-card or longer suit.
A 2оАї response requires the 1NT bidder to rebid 3оАІ, which may be passed with a
club bust, or responder may rebid 3оБђ with a diamond bust. Example:
     1NT вАФ 2оАї
      3оАІ вАФ Pass = club bust
		 вАФ 3оБђ = diamond bust (notrump opener passes).
Other responses to 1NT:
    1NT вАФ 3оАІ, 3оБђ = a six-card or longer suit and invitational to 3NT.
		 вАФ 3оБЂ, 3оАї = at least a six-card suit and slam interest
			 (otherwise, responder uses a transfer bid).
		 вАФ 4оАІ = Gerber, asking for aces. 4оАІ IS GERBER OVER ANY 1NT OR 2NT
			 BY PARTNER, INCLUDING A REBID OF 1NT OR 2NT. Responses 		
			 show the number of aces, by steps, just as over a Blackwood 		
			 4NT. 5оАІ is used to ask for kings.
        Ace Asking                                  King Asking
      1NT вАФ 4оАІ                                     1NT вАФ 4оАІ
      4оБђ = 0 or 4 aces                             4x    вАФ 5оАІ
      4оБЂ = 1 ace                                   5оБђ = 0 or 4 kings
      4оАї = 2 aces                                  5оБЂ = 1 king
      4NT = 3 aces                                 5оАї = 2 kings
				                                               5NT = 3 kings
If the player using Gerber makes any bid other than 5оАІ, that is to play
(including 4NT). Asking for kings guarantees that the partnership holds all the aces.
A direct raise of 1NT to 4NT is natural and invites 6NT. 4NT is slam invitational only
because 4оАІ is available as Gerber (to ask about aces).

                  INTERFERENCE AFTER A 1NT OPENING BID
If an opponent doubles, all conventional responses are вАЬon.вАЭ For example:
   1NT вАФ (Double) вАФ 2оАІ = Stayman
   1NT вАФ (Double) вАФ 2оБђ = transfer to hearts
If an opponent bids over your 1NT opener, conventional responses like Stayman
and transfers are вАЬoff.вАЭ Bids are natural except for a cuebid, which may be used with
game forcing strength as a substitute for Stayman.
If the opponents intervene over a conventional response, bids carry the same
meaning as if there were no intervention. A bid says, вАЬIвАЩm bidding voluntarily, so I
have a real fit with you.вАЭ

   1NT вАФ (Pass) вАФ 2оБђ вАФ (Double)
    2оБЂ = Real fit for hearts вАФ pass with only two hearts

                                          2
                     RESPONSES TO A 2NT OR A 3NT OPENING
Stayman and Jacoby transfers for the majors are used.
   2NT вАФ 3оАІ = Stayman.
		 вАФ 3оБђ, 3оБЂ = Jacoby transfers to hearts and spades, respectively.
		 вАФ 4оАІ = Gerber (ace asking).
		 вАФ 4NT = Invites a slam in notrump.
   3NT вАФ 4оАІ = Stayman.
		 вАФ 4оБђ, 4оБЂ = Jacoby transfers to hearts and spades, respectively.
      RESPONSES AND LATER BIDDING AFTER A 1оБЂ OR A 1оАї OPENING
1оБЂ and 1оАї openings show a five-card or longer suit. Responses:
   1 оБЂ вАФ 1оАї = at least four spades, 6 or more points. Tends to deny a heart fit.
		вАФ 1NT = 6вАУ9 points, denies four spades or three hearts. NOT forcing.
		вАФ 2оАІ, 2оБђ = 10 points or more, promises at least four of the suit.
		вАФ 2оБЂ = three-card or longer heart support; 6вАУ10 dummy points.
		вАФ 2NT = Game-forcing raise (Jacoby 2NT), 13+ dummy points. Asks		
            opener to show a short suit to help responder evaluate slam prospects.
            (see * below)
		вАФ 2оАї, 3оАІ, 3оБђ = strong jump shifts. Invites a slam.
		вАФ 3оБЂ = limit raise (10вАУ11 dummy points with three or more hearts).
		вАФ 3NT = 15вАУ17 HCP, balanced hand with two-card support for partner.
		вАФ 4оБЂ = usually five+ hearts, a singleton or void, and fewer than 10 HCP.
OpenerвАЩs rebids are natural and standard.
Rebids with a minimum hand (13вАУ15 points):
    Rebidding notrump at the lowest available level;
    Raising responderвАЩs suit at the lowest level (may have good three-card support);
    Rebidding a new suit at the one level or rebidding a new suit at the two level
     that is lower ranking than the opening suit (not reversing);
    Rebidding openerвАЩs suit at the lowest level.
Rebids with a medium hand (16вАУ18 points):
    Jump raise of responderвАЩs suit or jump rebid of openerвАЩs suit;
    Reverse in a new suit, i.e., bid a new suit at the two level which is higher ranking
    than the opening suit;
    Non-reverse bid in a new suit (this has the wide range of 13вАУ18 points).
With a maximum hand (19вАУ21 or 22 points) opener must make a very strong rebid:
    Jump in notrump;
    Double jump raise of responderвАЩs suit or double jump rebid of openerвАЩs suit;
    Jump shift in a new suit.

   * If responder jumps to 2NT over a 1оБЂ or 1оАї opening, that is Jacoby 2NT, asking
     opener to show a singleton or void. If opener has no short suit, he shows his
     hand strength;
		         1оБЂ вАФ 2NT
		         3оАІ, 3оБђ, 3оАї = singleton or void in that suit. Other bids deny a short suit.
		         4оБЂ = minimum hand.
		         3NT = medium hand (15вАУ17).
		         3оБЂ = maximum hand (18+)
		         4оАІ, 4оБђ = 2nd suit
Responder follows up by attempting to sign off in game, bidding 4NT Blackwood,
or cuebidding if still interested in trying to cooperate with opener in making the
slam decision.
                                            3
                      SUBSEQUENT BIDDING BY RESPONDER
If responder has bid a suit at the one level, he next determines whether he wishes
to sign off in a partscore, invite game, sign off in game, or force to game and get
more information about openerвАЩs hand.

Bids available for a sign-off in partscore: Pass, 1NT, 2 of a previously bid suit.
    1оБЂ вАФ 1оАї
    2оАІ вАФ Pass, 2оБЂ, 2оАї = 6вАУ9 points, sign-off in partscore.
Bids available for inviting game: 2NT, 3 of a previously bid suit:
    1оБЂ вАФ 1оАї
    2оБђ вАФ 2NT, 3оБђ, 3оБЂ, 3оАї = 10вАУ11 points, inviting game.
After opener rebids in a suit, a new suit by responder is forcing. If the new suit is the
fourth suit, the bid may be artificial/conventional.
     1оБЂ вАФ 1оАї
     2оАІ вАФ 2оБђ = one-round force, could be conventional.
However, after a 1NT rebid by opener, bids of a new suit at the next higher level are
non-forcing.
    1оБЂ вАФ 1оАї
    1NT вАФ 2оАІ, 2оБђ = non-forcing. Responder must jump shift to 3оАІ or 3оБђ
                      to force game.
Second-round forcing bids following a 1NT rebid by opener: a reverse or jump shift
into a new suit is a game force.
     1оАІ вАФ 1оБЂ
     1NT вАФ 2оАї or 3оБђ = game force.
Bids available for signing off in game.
    3NT, 4оБЂ, 4оАї, 5оАІ, 5оБђ.
If responder initially bids a new suit at the two level, the same rules apply EXCEPT
that a subsequent jump raise of openerвАЩs first suit to the THREE LEVEL is game
forcing вАФ responder should make a limit raise directly over the opening with 10вАУ11
points and at least three-card support:
     1оАї вАФ 2оАІ
     2оБЂ вАФ 2NT, 3оАІ, 3оБЂ = invitation to game (10вАУ11 points).
		 вАФ 2оАї = preference, not forcing. Responder has 11вАУ12 points and a 		
			                doubleton spade.
		 вАФ 3оБђ = game force, could be conventional.
		 вАФ 3оАї = game force.
NOTE: Responder promises to bid again if he responded with a new suit at the two
level unless openerвАЩs rebid is at the game level. This applies when responder is an
unpassed hand.
     1оАї вАФ 2оАІ
     2оБђ = forcing one round. Responder can limit his hand by bidding 2оАї, 2NT,
            3оАІ, or 3оБђ at this point. He should not pass, since opener could have 18
            points (just short of a jump shift rebid).




                                            4
                     RESPONSES TO A 1оАІ OR A 1оБђ OPENING
A 1оБђ opener suggests a four-card or longer suit, since 1оАІ is preferred on hands
where a three-card minor suit must be opened. The exception is a hand with 4вАУ4вАУ3вАУ2
shape: four spades, four hearts, three diamonds, and two clubs, which is opened 1оБђ.
Responses and later bidding generally follow the ideas set down in the previous
section. Bidding at the one level is up-the-line in principle. Responder needs more
support to raise (four to raise 1оБђ; five to raise 1оАІ, though one fewer will do in a
pinch in a competitive sequence). Responses of 2NT and 3NT are standard:
    1оАІ вАФ 2NT = 13вАУ15, game forcing
		 вАФ 3NT = 16вАУ18
There is no forcing minor-suit raise.
               A 2оАІ OPENING, RESPONSES, AND LATER BIDDING
A 2оАІ opening shows at least 22+ points, or the playing equivalent. Responses:
  2оАІ вАФ 2оБђ = conventional, could be вАЬwaitingвАЭ with a good hand not suited to a
       positive response.
		 вАФ 2оБЂ, 2оАї, 3оАІ, 3оБђ = natural and game forcing. At least a five-card suit
       and 8 points.
		 вАФ 2NT = a balanced 8 HCP.
If opener rebids 2NT after a 2оБђ response (showing 22вАУ24 points), the same
responses are used as over a 2NT opening:
     2оАІ вАФ 2оБђ
    2NT вАФ 3оАІ = Stayman.
		 вАФ 3оБђ, 3оБЂ = Jacoby transfers to hearts and spades, respectively.
		 вАФ 4оАІ = Gerber.
		 вАФ 4NT = inviting a slam in notrump.
If opener rebids a suit over a 2оБђ response, the bidding is forcing to 3 of openerвАЩs
major or 4 of openerвАЩs minor.
     2оАІ вАФ 2оБђ
     2оБЂ вАФ 2оАї
     3оБЂ = not forcing.
           SEQUENCE AFTER A WEAK TWO-BID OF 2оБђ, 2оБЂ OR 2оАї
A weak two-bid shows a six-card suit of reasonable quality and 5вАУ11 HCP. On rare
occasions it may be a very good five-card suit. It is possible to open a weak two with a
poor seven-card suit (not good enough to open with at the three level). Responses:
    A 2NT response is forcing, showing game interest. (This applies also if the
    opponents intervene with a double or a bid.) Opener rebids his suit with a
    minimum weak two (5вАУ8 points). With a maximum hand opener bids another
    suit to show a вАЬfeatureвАЭ (ace or king in that suit); lacking a feature, he raises to
    3NT and lets responder place the contract.
    Any raise of openerвАЩs suit is to play and could be preemptive (weak). A 3NT
    response is also to play.
вАЬRONFвАЭ on the card means вАЬRaise Only Non-Force.вАЭ A new-suit response is
forcing one round and shows at least a five-card suit. Opener should raise a major
suit response with a three-card fit, or perhaps with a doubleton honor.
With no fit for responderвАЩs suit, opener rebids:
    With a minimum weak two-bid (5вАУ8 points), rebid the suit at the lowest level.
    With a maximum weak two-bid, name a new suit or bid notrump.

                                           5
                                  OPENING PREEMPTS
Opposite an unpassed partner, an opening 3 or 4-level call in a suit tends toward
sound at equal or unfavorable vulnerability. Vulnerable against not, you should be
within two tricks of your bid (i.e. with likely distribution of the remaining cards in your
suit). When the vulnerability is equal, you should be within three tricks of your bid. At
favorable vulnerability, the preempt tends to be lighter, so you should be within four
tricks of your bid.
                                    SLAM BIDDING
Blackwood 4NT is used to ask for aces. Responses show the number of aces by
steps. 5NT is then used to ask for kings; 5NT guarantees that the partnership holds
all four aces.
				                                                 вАФ    вАФ вАФ
 вАФ  вАФ вАФ                                              вАФ    вАФ 4NT
 вАФ  вАФ 4NT                                            5x   вАФ 5NT
 5оАІ = 0 or 4 aces                                    6оАІ	¬≠ = 0 or 4 kings
 5оБђ = 1 ace                                          6оБђ = 1 king
 5оБЂ = 2 aces                                         6оБЂ = 2 kings
 5оАї = 3 aces                                         6оАї = 3 kings
A jump to 5NT (and some 5NT bids when the auction is at the five level) is вАЬGrand
Slam Force,вАЭ asking partner to bid a grand slam with two of the three top honors in
the agreed suit;
     5NT вАФ 6 of the agreed suit = fewer than two top honors (A, K or Q).
         вАФ 7 of the agreed suit = two of the three top honors.
                                 DEFENSIVE BIDDING
Overcalls show 8вАУ16 points (double and bid the long suit with a stronger hand).
The only forcing response is a cuebid of openerвАЩs suit, asking the overcaller about
the quality of his overcall:
  (1оБђ) вАФ 1оАї вАФ (Pass) вАФ 2оБђ
  (Pass) вАФ 2оАї = minimum overcall.
		       вАФ other = extra strength (11 or 12 points minimum).
A 1NT overcall shows 15вАУ18 points and a balanced hand (preferably a stopper in
openerвАЩs suit). No conventional responses are used by the responder to the 1NT
overcall except 2оАІ, which is Stayman.
A jump overcall of 2NT shows at least 5вАУ5 in the lowest two unbid suits.
Jump overcalls are preemptive, showing the same value as an opening bid at the
same level:
   (1оБђ) вАФ 2оАї = a hand that would open a weak two-bid in spades.
		          вАФ 3оАІ = a hand that would open 3оАІ.
A cuebid overcall when the opponents have bid two suits is natural in either suit.
A cuebid overcall when the opponents have bid only one suit is a Michaels cuebid,
showing a 5вАУ5 two-suiter (or more distributional). If the opening is in a minor suit,
the cuebid shows the majors; if the opening is in a major, the cuebid shows the
other major and an unspecified minor.
    (1оБђ) вАФ 2оБђ = at least 5вАУ5 in the majors, 8 points or more.
    (1оАї) вАФ 2оАї = at least 5вАУ5 in hearts and a minor; 10 points or more.

                                             6
 Responder may bid 2NT over a major suit cuebid to ask for partnerвАЩs minor.
   (1оБЂ) вАФ 2оБЂ вАФ (Pass) вАФ 2NT (asks for the minor).
   (Pass) вАФ 3оАІ = club suit.
		        вАФ 3оБђ = diamond suit.
A reopening bid means much the same as a direct seat bid, though it can be lighter
at the minimum end. A reopening 1NT after an opponent has opened shows 10вАУ15
points. This is a wide range, but there will not usually be a game on for you.
A double is for takeout over an opening partscore bid (4оБђ or lower); penalty over
opening game bids (4оБЂ or higher). A below-game jump response to a takeout
double is invitational. To force, responder cuebids openerвАЩs suit.
Versus an opening preempt, an overcall in a suit or notrump is natural; a cuebid is
Michaels.
                              COMPETITIVE BIDDING
There is almost an endless variety of possible sequences, so it pays to have
simple guidelines to prevent bidding misunderstandings.
Bids mean the same things they meant without the intervening bid. Still, it is
sometimes necessary to pick a bid that would normally have been a second choice
without the overcall:
    1оБђ вАФ (Pass) вАФ 1оАї вАФ (2оАІ)
    2оАї with оАїJ 4 3 оБЂA 8 7 5 оБђA Q J 4 оАІJ 3 (rebid 1NT if RHO has passed)
Cuebidding right-hand opponentвАЩs suit shows values for game without clear
direction for the moment. This is often used to show a game-forcing raise:
     1оАї вАФ (2оАІ) вАФ 3оАІ = game force; usually a raise.
The negative double is used through 2оАї, promising four cards (at least) in an unbid
major. Bidding a major at the two level or higher shows 11 or more points and a five-
card or longer suit.
    1оАІ вАФ (1оБђ) вАФ Double = 4вАУ4 or better in the majors.
    1оБђ вАФ (1оБЂ) вАФ Double = exactly four spades (1оАї promises five).
    1оБђ вАФ (1оАї) вАФ Double = four hearts and 6+ points or five hearts
             and 5вАУ10 points.
If RHO makes a takeout double:
     1оБђ вАФ (Double) вАФ 1оБЂ, 1оАї = forcing, point count not limited.
		      вАФ 2оАІ = non-forcing (6вАУ10 points, usually a six-card suit).
		      вАФ 2NT = limit raise (at least 10 points) or better.
		      вАФ Redouble = 10 points or more, but it is better to make a more 		
           descriptive bid of 1оБЂ, 1оАї, or 2NT with the appropriate hand.
		      вАФ 3оБђ = preemptive, good trump support but fewer than 10 points.
A responderвАЩs jump shift after a double is to play:
    1оБђ вАФ (Double) вАФ 2оБЂ, 2оАї, 3оАІ = six-plus card suit, like a weak two-bid or
            preemptive three-bid.
A redouble can have one of three meanings:
To play if:
Your side is at the four level or higher:
    4оАї вАФ (Double) вАФ Redouble = Penalty;


                                          7
 The opponents double a conventional bid:
    1NT вАФ (Pass) вАФ 2оБђ вАФ (Double)
Redouble = Penalty, good diamond suit;

A good hand if their double is for takeout:
    1оАї вАФ (Double) вАФ Redouble = 10+ points;
SOS, requesting a different suit, if your side is doubled for penalty in a trump suit
at the three level or lower:
     1оБђ вАФ (Pass) вАФ Pass вАФ (Double)
     Pass вАФ (Pass) вАФ Redouble = SOS, responder can support at least two of the
              unbid suits.
Unless otherwise noted elsewhere, any bid or double by an opponent cancels
a convention intended for non-competitive sequences.
    Examples: 1оБЂ вАФ 2оАї вАФ 2NT = natural (12вАУ14 HCP).
		            2оАІ вАФ Double вАФ 2оБђ = natural and positive.
If the opponents use a convention (such as Michaels or the unusual notrump), you
can double to show at least 10 points, or you can cuebid one of their shown suits
to force to game.
     1оАї вАФ (2оАї) вАФ 3оБЂ = game force.
		              вАФ Double = at least 10 points, probably balanced.
                        DEFENSIVE LEADS AND SIGNALS
Defensive signals when following suit or discarding are вАЬhigh encourages, low
discourages.вАЭ And вАЬHigh-low is an even number, low-high is odd when count is
given.вАЭ
Leads are fourth best. From three low cards against a suit contract lead low; against
a notrump contract lead high. From four cards or longer lead fourth best. The one
systemic exception is leading from a suit of four or more cards without an honor. In
this instance lead the second highest card.
Leads are top of touching honors and top from an interior sequence. Against suits,
the ace is led from A K x.
NOTE: When playing the ACBL SAYC:
You may add defenses to the opponentsвАЩ conventions (e.g., unusual vs. unusual, and
Mathe over big club). Put these convention-defenses in the section вАЬDefenses vs.
OppвАЩs ConventionsвАЭ on the left-hand side of the convention card.




#170358                                                                       SP3 (bk)
Printed in USA                                                               Rev. 1/06

teaching.tradeoff: There is no special tradeoff here вАФ these bids keep their usual natural meanings
teaching.principle: When no major fit exists, raise notrump to the level your combined HCP supports вАФ 25 total for game.

commonMistakes.text: Don't jump to three notrump with a worthless doubleton вАФ consider whether a suit contract might play better
commonMistakes.reason: Notrump games need controls on the side suits; a bare doubleton in responder's hand can concede the first trick and set up a long suit for the defence.

commonMistakes.text: With a flat invitational hand, raise to two notrump rather than stretching to game with marginal values
commonMistakes.reason: Opposite a minimum one notrump opening, game is poor on barely-invitational values; responder should let opener accept or decline.

commonMistakes.text: Treat one club as possibly three cards in a four-four-three-two or four-three-three-three hand; do not play responder for four-plus clubs
commonMistakes.reason: With four-four in the majors and three-two in the minors, opener must bid one club because one diamond would promise four; the short-club convention keeps the opening natural without misrepresenting club length.

clauses.description: Hand does not qualify for a Strong 2C opening
clauses.rationale: Classic Goren / SAYC: Strong 2C outranks any natural one-level opening whenever the hand qualifies (22+ HCP balanced or 9+ playing tricks).

teachingLabel.name: 15 to 17
teachingLabel.summary: Open 1NT showing a balanced hand within the system HCP range

clauses.rationale: Deny a 5-card spade suit so 1S wins on any 5+ spade hand.
Deny a 5-card heart suit so 1H wins on any 5+ heart hand.
SAYC opens 1C on 3-3 minors and on minor hands where diamonds are shorter than clubs. 1D is preferred on 4-4 minors, longer diamonds, and the 4=4=3=2 shape.

clauses.description: Hand does not qualify for a Strong 2C opening
clauses.rationale: Classic Goren / SAYC: Strong 2C outranks any natural one-level opening whenever the hand qualifies (22+ HCP balanced or 9+ playing tricks).

teachingLabel.name: 1вЩ£ opening
teachingLabel.summary: Open 1вЩ£ with opening values, no 5-card major, and minor shape where diamonds are not preferred

clauses.rationale: Deny a 5-card spade suit so 1S wins on any 5+ spade hand.
Deny a 5-card heart suit so 1H wins on any 5+ heart hand.
SAYC opens 1D with four or more diamonds at least as long as clubs (covering 4-4 minors), and on the balanced 4=4=3=2 shape where partner is told clubs could be three.

clauses.description: Hand does not qualify for a Strong 2C opening
clauses.rationale: Classic Goren / SAYC: Strong 2C outranks any natural one-level opening whenever the hand qualifies (22+ HCP balanced or 9+ playing tricks).

teachingLabel.name: 1вЩ¶ opening
teachingLabel.summary: Open 1вЩ¶ with opening values, no 5-card major, and minor shape where diamonds are preferred

clauses.rationale: SAYC opens 1H when hearts is strictly longer than spades, or when spades is below a 5-card major; with 5-5 majors, 1S wins as the higher of equal long suits.

clauses.description: Hand does not qualify for a Strong 2C opening
clauses.rationale: Classic Goren / SAYC: Strong 2C outranks any natural one-level opening whenever the hand qualifies (22+ HCP balanced or 9+ playing tricks).

teachingLabel.name: 1вЩ• opening
teachingLabel.summary: Open 1вЩ• with opening values, a 5-card or longer heart suit, and hearts at least as long as spades except for the 5-5 tie

clauses.description: Hand does not qualify for a Strong 2C opening
clauses.rationale: Classic Goren / SAYC: Strong 2C outranks any natural one-level opening whenever the hand qualifies (22+ HCP balanced or 9+ playing tricks).

teachingLabel.name: 1вЩ† opening
teachingLabel.summary: Open 1вЩ† with opening values and a 5-card or longer spade suit

teachingLabel.name: NT invite
teachingLabel.summary: Invite game in notrump with no 4-card or 5-card major

teachingLabel.name: 3NT game
teachingLabel.summary: Bid game in notrump with game-forcing values and no 4-card or 5-card major

summaryCard.trigger: No specialized agreement applies and the auction stays natural
summaryCard.partnership: This module is intentionally an umbrella baseline, not a full continuation encyclopedia. Use it as the natural layer underneath the more specific response and opening modules.

whenToUse.gloss: Use the natural one-level openings when you have opening values and no attached convention changes the meaning of the call.
Use the natural notrump raises here when responder has the right invite-or-game strength opposite one notrump and no major-suit route is better.

interference.reason: This module is an umbrella for baseline natural actions, not a dedicated competitive treatment. Use the competitive modules for interference-specific agreements.

items.recommendation: This axis does not apply: opposite a one notrump opening, a weak responder passes, bids Stayman, or uses Jacoby transfers instead of a natural notrump raise.
items.note: Natural-bids covers only the invite and game notrump raises; the weak-responder row is handled by pass and by the Stayman and transfer modules.

items.recommendation: Without a major-suit convention route, invite with two notrump.
items.note: Use this only when Stayman and transfers are off the table.

items.recommendation: Without a major-suit convention route, place the hand in three notrump.
items.note: Game-going natural notrump raises stay here; the specialized modules cover major-suit searches.

relatedLinks.discriminator: Use Stayman when the natural one notrump response should become a major-suit ask instead of a direct natural raise.
Garbage Stayman escapes a weak unbalanced responder to a major-suit partscore instead of leaving the hand in an unsuitable one notrump.
Transfers replace natural direct bids when responder has a five-card major and wants opener to declare.
Weak two openings are specialized preemptive actions; this module covers the natural baseline when that treatment is not in play.
Strong two clubs replaces ordinary natural openings when the hand is too strong for the baseline structure.

definitions.description: SAYC prefers 1D over 1C: four-plus diamonds with diamonds at least as long as clubs (covers 4-4 minors and longer diamonds), or the 4=4=3=2 balanced exception.
1H takes priority over 1S: spades is not a 5-card major, or hearts is strictly longer than spades.

Hand does not qualify for a Strong 2C opening вАФ fewer than 22 HCP balanced AND fewer than nine playing tricks. Every natural one-level opening requires this because Strong 2C outranks any natural one-level opening whenever the hand qualifies.
